poster_title: LifelongAgentBench: Evaluating LLM Agents as Lifelong Learners
authors: Junhao Zheng, Xidi Cai, Qiuke Li, Duzhen Zhang, Zhong-Zhi Li, Yingying Zhang, Le Song, Qianli Ma

content: LLMs have transformed NLP tasks, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
Research focus has shifted to LLM-based agents for dynamic environments.
Current benchmarks lack evaluation of lifelong learning capabilities.
Label inaccuracies and poor reproducibility hinder practical adoption.
LifelongAgentBench addresses these issues with a unified benchmark.
LLM-based agents lack memory and incremental knowledge accumulation.
Existing benchmarks do not evaluate lifelong learning.
Prior benchmarks have issues with label accuracy and reproducibility.
LifelongAgentBench evaluates lifelong learning in LLM agents.
Includes skill-grounded, interdependent tasks in three environments.
Key features: automatic label verification, reproducibility, and modular extensibility.
panels.figure_caption: Overview of LifelongAgentBench: a unified dataset and evaluation framework with skill-grounded tasks.

content: Tasks test agents' skill acquisition, transfer, and stable performance.
Experiments use various LLM backbones to evaluate experience replay.
Group self-consistency mechanism is also evaluated.
Conventional experience replay is limited by irrelevant information and context length.
Group self-consistency mechanism significantly improves lifelong learning performance.
LifelongAgentBench provides a robust platform for evaluating LLM agents.
Group self-consistency is a promising approach for lifelong learning.
Future work will explore different model sizes and architectures.
